free_text: Measurements of proton induced gamma-ray emission cross
sections on Al from 2.5 MeV to 4.1 MeV.
A 29 mug/cm**2 Al film evaporated on a self-supporting
102 mug/cm**2 Ag
experimental results were normalized by the Rutherford
elastic backscattering of protons from Ag.
two HPGe coaxial detectors, of nominal
efficiency 50% and 25%, and energy resolution 2.0 keV
for 1.33 MeV.
The elastic scattered protons were measured by a
Si-PIN diode detector,10 mm**2 area, 300 mum thickness
and 13 keV FWHM energy resolution.
SB
.Table data were received from Dr.M.Chiari
The HVEE 3 MV Tandetron accelerator
at the INFN LABEC laboratory in Florence
Dead time corrections were always less than 15% for the
gamma-detector, whereas they were negligible for the
particle detector (<0.5%).
The estimated uncertainties in the absolute values of
the differential cross sections are
.Error in gamma-ray area.
.Error in p+Ag elastic peak area.
.The contributions to the error in the
cross-section value from rutherford cross-section on
Ag.
.The uncertainty of Al to Ag atomic
.The uncerainty of particle detector solid
angle.
.The uncertainty of HPGe  detector absolute
efficiency.
.The statistical errors from the peak areas,
including background subtraction
.Table data were received from Dr.M.Chiari
